category.name: Join Documents
category.preferred_workflow: Use native Aspose.Words document append and insert APIs directly with local sample documents, explicit ImportFormatMode, and documented mail-merge insertion workflows when needed.

strict_api_bans: Do not assume source documents or folders already exist.
Do not invent unsupported document-join APIs.
Do not append or insert a node into an invalid location.
Do not skip saving the requested merged output.
canonical_patterns: Create all required local source documents before joining them.
Use AppendDocument for whole-document joins and specify ImportFormatMode explicitly.
Use InsertDocument or InsertDocumentInline only when inserting at a specific cursor position or inside a documented mail-merge workflow.
Validate that the merged output contains content from all expected source documents.
nullable_warning_prevention: Initialize all non-nullable reference type properties to avoid CS8618 warnings.
Avoid CS8600, CS8602, and CS8604 by guarding maybe-null values before dereference or assignment.
Declare nullable locals when a value may legitimately be null and null-check before use.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Examples assume source DOC or DOCX files already exist.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Create all source documents locally inside the example before joining them.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Code invents unsupported APIs or uses append logic where insertion is required.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use AppendDocument for whole-document appends and InsertDocument or InsertDocumentInline only for real insertion scenarios.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Runtime failures due to inserting imported sections or nodes into an invalid parent location.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Import content into valid containers only and never append a node to itself.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Document insertion during mail merge is treated like a plain append or uses invented merge helpers.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use the documented mail-merge event-handler insertion workflow with InsertDocument or InsertDocumentInline.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: The example saves output but does not prove the result contains content from all expected sources.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Validate using actual markers, paragraph text, section counts, or output file existence.

examples.task: Load a DOCX template, insert a source DOCX at a bookmark, and save the result as PDF.
Load a DOCX, insert a PDF‑converted DOCX at a bookmark, and save the merged document as ODT.
Load a password‑protected DOCX, insert a source DOCX at a bookmark, then remove protection and save as DOCX.
Protect a destination DOCX with password before inserting a source DOCX, then remove protection and save as DOCX.
Protect a destination DOCX with read‑only restriction before appending source DOCX, then remove restriction and save as PDF.
Use DocumentBuilder.MoveToBookmark to navigate to a bookmark named “Content”, then insert a source DOCX with KeepSourceFormatting.
Insert a DOCX at a bookmark inside a header, then save the document as DOCX preserving header formatting.
Insert a DOCX at a bookmark inside a table cell, then export document to ODT preserving table structure.
Insert a DOCX at multiple bookmarks named Header and Footer, then export the final document to HTML format.
Use DocumentBuilder.InsertDocument to insert a DOCX at the current cursor position with KeepSourceFormatting, saving as HTML.
Use DocumentBuilder.InsertDocument with ImportFormatMode.UseDestinationStyles to insert a DOCX into a styled template, then save as HTML.
Insert a DOCX during a find‑replace operation that matches the word “INSERTME”, then save as DOCX.
Use FindReplaceOptions to replace the phrase “PLACEHOLDER” with a DOCX document, then save as ODT.
Use FindReplaceOptions with a ReplacingCallback to insert a DOCX whenever phrase “INSERT_HERE” is found, then save as DOCX.
Insert a DOCX during a find‑replace operation that replaces an XML tag, then save the document as DOCX.
Set MailMerge.InsertDocument event to inject a DOCX for each data row, and generate a combined PDF file.
Insert a DOCX during mail merge for record, then apply ImportLanguageInfo to preserve language settings, save as PDF.
Use ImportLanguageInfo option to preserve language settings when appending a source DOCX to a destination DOCX.
Manually import specific tables from a source DOCX using NodeImporter and insert them into a destination DOCX.
Use NodeImporter to import only paragraph nodes from a source DOCX and insert them after a specific bookmark.
Append an RTF document to an existing DOCX using UseDestinationStyles, then export the combined file to DOCX.
Append a DOCX containing custom styles to a destination DOCX, using ImportFormatMode.UseDestinationStyles, then export to PDF.
Append a DOCX generated from a mail merge operation to an existing PDF‑converted DOCX, preserving destination styles.
Append a DOCX with video files to a destination DOCX, ensuring videos remain functional after saving as PDF.
Append a DOCX containing footnotes to a destination DOCX and ensure footnote numbering continues, then save as PDF.
Append a DOCX containing a bibliography to a research paper DOCX, then update fields and export to PDF.
Combine three ODT files into one DOCX, applying ImportFormatMode.KeepSourceFormatting, then save as DOCX.
Combine a DOCX and an ODT file into a single PDF, using ImportFormatMode.KeepSourceFormatting for the ODT content.
Combine multiple DOCX files into a single PDF, ensuring each source document retains its original formatting.
Append multiple DOCX files in a loop, each with different ImportFormatMode settings, and output combined file as PDF.
Batch process a directory of DOCX files, appending each to a master document and saving result as PDF.
Batch append DOCX files from multiple subfolders into a single master document, applying ImportFormatMode.UseDestinationStyles, then export to PDF.
Use DocumentBuilder to insert a DOCX at end of sections in a destination DOCX, then export to DOCX.
Validate that all inserted sections contain correct page numbers by iterating through destination document nodes after insertion.
Append a DOCX generated from a web service to an existing DOCX, then encrypt final PDF with password.
Append a DOCX generated from a REST API to an existing DOCX, then encrypt final PDF with password.
